$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 2 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: The crown does not attend hearings. While the paddock weighed its paperwork and the hearing-room door swung like a pit gate, the yellow-and-red garage went on with the only argument it recognises: the MADONNA 456 left the box immaculate, the PALM 190 V10 never raised its voice, and A. Senna stretched the deepest pole ledger the almanac keeps, Saturday after Saturday, each read aloud to the grandstands. A. Asselin took everyone foolish enough to look past him. The winter's chequebook noise reached the garage and was filed under weather. Serenity, the house holds, is the advantage no protest touches.
team.madonna.objective: Remain above the quarrel and keep the pole ledger growing while the paddock argues indoors.
team.madonna.definingEvent: A season of Saturdays, each one stretching the deepest pole ledger the almanac has ever kept.
team.madonna.carryForward: The era's first chequebook winter arrives; the crown watches the raid the way it watches weather.

team.firenze.summary: The wound was dressed in wine and gold and shown to no one. Three engineers walked to the chrome garage over one winter, and the house called it theft in private, Tuesday's business in public, then rebuilt its technical office and paraded it before the tifosi at Italy like a bloodline that had not died. F. Elssler said nothing and banked everything within reach, the FIRENZE 500 turned out mirror-bright as ever, the FIRENZE 99 V12 still the paddock's only aria. I. Germi conducted the grandstands while N. Jones shouted at them. Breeding, the house insists, cannot be subpoenaed.
team.firenze.objective: Bleed quietly, rebuild publicly, and keep the ice between Millions and the crown.
team.firenze.definingEvent: Italy: the rebuilt technical office paraded before the tifosi, wounded pride turned into pageantry.
team.firenze.carryForward: The cold war is now the era's permanent weather, and the house keeps its ice.

team.millions.summary: The raid came before the first flag: three Firenze engineers walked into the chrome garage, the budget tripled, and the paddock's newest cold war had its opening shot. The MILLIONS 189 ran loud all season, N. Jones trading three boasts for every one of I. Germi's arias, G. Alberti carrying the #6 with old manners above the roar. The DICK MD V10 drank like a fortune and paid like one too, some Sundays. The year's truest signing, the garage admits, was not a driver but a man who reads rulebooks. Money had bought speed; now it had bought interpretation.
team.millions.objective: Weaponise the raid: turn poached knowledge and a tripled budget into a title argument.
team.millions.definingEvent: The winter walk of three Firenze engineers into the chrome garage, hiring or theft depending on the motorhome.
team.millions.carryForward: A cold war that will outlast the era, and a rulebook reader on the payroll.

team.bestowal.summary: While the paddock weighed its paperwork, the given house simply raced, which this season read as eccentricity. The BESTOWAL 167's sealed VAPOR DN, serviced by the combine's grey men, was immune by design to the year's favourite indoor sport, and the drawing office logged the long-straight data nobody else had time for. M. Blume posted his metronome laps; A. Picos converted what Sundays offered. The 167 stayed heavy in the slow stuff and correct in the wet, and the house stayed what it is: patient, funded, and certain the next rulebook would pay for its kind of speed.
team.bestowal.objective: Keep the gift above the paper war and bank the correctness the loud garages waste.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: The season's quietest stance: not one sheet of protest paper filed, not one hearing attended.
team.bestowal.carryForward: A deepening file of straight-line data and a growing lobby for a horsepower formula.

team.blanche.summary: The white cars were protested once this year; the garage framed the complaint. It came from the gunmetal end of LEVEL B, the scrutineers went over the BLANCHE 582 with lamps and gauges, and found, of course, nothing: the whitecoats had logged the bolts before the officials arrived. J. Herbin kept his metronome running at the top of the class, P. White kept believing, and the DELTA 103 V10 kept its honest, unanswerable beat. In a season the midfield spent indoors, Blanche raced. The door to LEVEL A stayed shut, but the knocking was now being heard upstairs.
team.blanche.objective: Answer every summons with a clean car and every rival with a clean pass.
team.blanche.definingEvent: The Tyrant complaint examined and dismissed, the whitest verdict scrutineering ever returned.
team.blanche.carryForward: A feud now sharpened to a point, and an upstairs audience learning the knocking's rhythm.

team.tyrant.summary: The boot camp does not file paperwork; it files dents. The iron fist settled its LEVEL B arguments the old way all year, M. Hamano ice-calm on the crossovers, G. Gould using the walls as reference points, and when the garage tried the establishment's indoor sport, one complaint aimed at Blanche's bodywork, the scrutineers found nothing and the pit wall pretended the episode was reconnaissance. The TYRANT 548's brakes remained the cruellest in the class; the LIZZIE 24 V8 remained the limit. The door queue at LEVEL B was becoming a crowd, and Tyrant hates crowds it cannot intimidate.
team.tyrant.objective: Settle the LEVEL B argument with knuckles, and keep Blanche in denting range.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: The failed complaint against the white car, filed once and never spoken of again.
team.tyrant.carryForward: A door queue forming at LEVEL B, and the pit wall's hatred of queues.

team.losel.summary: The carbon-copy affair was, to the wind-tunnel house, a data problem, and the data said what the hearing rooms refused to: two philosophies had converged on the same bones, and legality is not the same thing as originality. The report was politely ignored, which the garage prefers. E. Pacheco drove his cold equations, W. Dehehe kept the blue needle steady, and the LOSEL 125's VAPOR DNPQ V8 gave documented, openable honesty where the sister cars gave lawyers. The conclusion the winter report underlined twice: the paddock argues about what it cannot measure. Losel measures.
team.losel.objective: Extract truth from the copy affair and turn the midfield's noise into private numbers.
team.losel.definingEvent: The ignored report: two philosophies, one bloodline, no crime the gauges could name.
team.losel.carryForward: A private measurement of both sister cars, filed for the day the feud turns fast.

team.may.summary: Nobody at May has ever filed a protest, because nobody at May has ever read that far into the rulebook. While the sport argued indoors, G. Turner kept the MAY 555 in the sunshine, waving on the warm-up lap, blooming on the warm afternoons, and turning the grey season into the green car's steadiest climb yet. The LORRY 32 V8 outlived, as ever, the racecars around it and asked nothing. The hothouse flowers above remained out of reach and increasingly, the garage noted, within view. Spring's ledger balanced: a young team, a full season, and not one grey hair earned.
team.may.objective: Climb while the others argue, and keep the sunshine budget balanced.
team.may.definingEvent: The grey season's steadiest green climb, no paperwork filed, no grey hairs earned.
team.may.carryForward: Within view of the hothouse flowers, and still growing toward the glass.

team.bullets.summary: Four times the sister-chassis garage dragged the BULLETS 560 before the stewards, and four times the car was stripped to the bare tub and sent back without a stain on it. G. Ceara raced between hearings like a man collecting evidence, and the wins-per-start argument against the aristocrats grew sharper with every flag. The featherweight build survived its second examination year; the gearbox still lived on its highwire; the LIZZIE 24 V8 sang harder than paid to. The lock-up's noticeboard now carries four dismissed protests, framed. The paddock calls the feud its favourite sport. Bullets calls it advertising.
team.bullets.objective: Turn four hearings into four acquittals and keep raiding between appointments.
team.bullets.definingEvent: The autumn collapse of the fourth hearing: nothing proven, everything remembered.
team.bullets.carryForward: A grudge hardened into the ladder's longest-running blood sport, and a noticeboard of framed dismissals.

team.dardan.summary: The spear became a briefcase for a season, and the clan hated the weight of it. E. Bellini fronted the paperwork personally from the San Marino opener, four hearings in one campaign, each ending alike: both cars stripped to the tub, nothing anyone could name, the grudge fed and never settled. The DARDAN 700 raced hard between appointments, K. Alfven banking the points the war kept interrupting, the VAPOR DNPQ V8 giving documented honesty as a standing rebuke. The clan's pride is that it never knelt. The clan's ledger says the kneeling was done by the verdicts.
team.dardan.objective: Win in the hearing room what the track would not give, and keep the spear blooded between hearings.
team.dardan.definingEvent: The fourth and final protest collapsing in autumn, the grudge now the grid's favourite public sport.
team.dardan.carryForward: A blood feud with its own folklore, and a clan that has learned paperwork cannot kill the loaded gun.

team.linden.summary: The old green car was the one machine in the paddock never summoned, never stripped, never even gossiped about, which suits the tree. M. Moreau watched the hearing year like a forester, deepened the longest tenure the championship keeps, and filed away things heard in anger, because old roots remember what young storms forget. The LINDEN LN198 finished everything, the LORRY 32 V8 seasoned another year past breaking, and the harvest came in on time. When the paddock's paper war bored itself quiet, the tree was still standing exactly where it was planted, and everyone noticed without saying so.
team.linden.objective: Finish everything, remember everything, and outlast the noise.
team.linden.definingEvent: The season's quietest record: the only garage in the paddock the stewards never once called.
team.linden.carryForward: A store of remembered angers, and an elder statesman the paddock does not yet know it needs.

team.minarae.summary: The on-ramp's answer to the protest year was to keep racing through it. B. Miller turned the distracted midfield into open country, brake-late through the canyons while everyone else watched the hearing-room door, and J. Nono kept eating hairpins, the promise still louder than the shelf. The MINARAE 594 took the year's bumps honestly, and the SEGA SG1000 V8 ran the full sixteen rounds without once asking a question a mechanic could not answer. Horsepower stayed the ceiling. The scouts' notebooks filled faster this year: nothing makes a climber visible like a paddock looking the other way.
team.minarae.objective: Race through the noise and make both names unavoidable in the scouts' notebooks.
team.minarae.definingEvent: Miller's canyon raids on a midfield too busy watching the hearing-room door to defend itself.
team.minarae.carryForward: Two notebooks full of underlinings and a sixteen-round engine that never asked a hard question.

team.rigel.summary: The museum watched the paper war with the faint smile of a house that settled its disputes with wrenches. Down at the floor the season passed as it always passes: R. Cotman faithful in the #26, T. Chardin courtly in the #27, the RIGEL 3000 beautiful, brittle, and intermittently willing. The LORRY 32 V8 honoured most of its superstitions. One points finish became the year's festival, toasted from the good bottle and entered in the ledger by calligraphic hand. The paddock's loud new grievances interest it less than the old ones. A faded star, it maintains, is still a star.
team.rigel.objective: Keep the ghost above the abyss and add one festival to the ledger.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The points finish toasted from the good bottle and entered in the ledger by calligraphic hand.
team.rigel.carryForward: The old grievances outlast the new ones; the star keeps its distance and its pride.

team.comet.summary: The paper war never once mentioned the matte-black car, and the garage finds the insult restful. E. Tornio went on saying nothing and driving everything, the COMET 323 producing two flying laps of remembered glory on Saturdays when the LIZZIE 24 V8 felt generous, and the customary plume on Sundays when it did not. The basement quarrel with Cool over which team peaked hardest and fell furthest ran all year, settled by nothing, fuelled by pride. The spares shelf stayed bare and the highlight reel stayed framed. Hope is a maintenance schedule, and this season it was kept.
team.comet.objective: Burn bright twice a weekend and keep the basement quarrel alive.
team.comet.definingEvent: Another Saturday flyer, two laps of the old light, before the Sunday plume wrote the usual ending.
team.comet.carryForward: The quarrel with Cool unsettled, the reel still framed, the schedule kept.

team.orchis.summary: A cold spring is a kind of gift, and the greenhouse unwrapped it. In the season's grey, blustery stretches the ORCHIS 056 flowered, C. Tegner reading the skies with new confidence, timing his runs to the cool windows when the MISFIRE 50 V8 could breathe, and twice the pale car stole points from garages ten times its budget. The hot afternoons wilted it on schedule, though the ledge above Zeroforce crumbled further. But the driver's posture at the pit wall had changed: he had stopped watching stopwatches and started watching clouds. The frost gambler was learning.
team.orchis.objective: Unwrap every cool window the season offers and keep the ledge from crumbling.
team.orchis.definingEvent: Two cloudy-day points steals, timed to the sky rather than the stopwatch.
team.orchis.carryForward: A driver who watches clouds now, and a paddock that will learn to watch him.

team.zeroforce.summary: The hearing rooms of the world sent no invitations to the last garage on the road, which was fine; the postage budget would not cover a reply. P. Klinger kept the blacked-out ZEROFORCE 231 circulating through the loudest season of argument, qualifying when the car agreed, finishing when it really agreed, staying off the bottom through sheer persistence. The LORRY 32 V8 went, as it goes, without opinion. The crew's verdict on the protest year, delivered over cold coffee, is unprintable. The floor held. At this address, the floor holding is the season reviewed, approved, and filed.
team.zeroforce.objective: Circulate, persist, stay off the bottom, and answer no summons but the flag's.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: The floor holding through the loudest argument the sport has staged indoors.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: The verdict delivered over cold coffee, unprintable, and the lights still on.

team.joke.summary: Summoned before the stewards at mid-season, L. Dufay did what no defendant in the championship's history had done: he delivered his defence as a comedy routine, with the timing of a man who has played worse rooms. The tribunal laughed, the fine stood, and the #16 survived another year, which was the entire point. On track the usual honest dishonesty continued: sandbagged Saturdays, buried throttles on Sundays, the POND V8's marine indifference to drama. The establishment has stopped asking whether the clowning is serious. It has started asking whether the lap times are jokes too, and dislikes the answer.
team.joke.objective: Survive the summons, keep the seat, and turn the fine into folklore.
team.joke.definingEvent: Dufay's defence delivered as a comedy routine: the tribunal laughed, the fine stood, the legend was born.
team.joke.carryForward: A seat bought with punchlines, and an establishment that no longer knows what to file the #16 under.

team.lares.summary: The hearth had its busiest winter of hospitality: after every hearing the weary men found their way to the warm garage with the brass crest. P. Arai grew the finishing record in the old manner, no drama, no summons, no stains, the RAM V12 turning its geological laps while the sport shouted indoors. The neighbourly feud with Bullets ran warm as ever, snarling on track, cards exchanged after. Points came when the argument consumed the fancier garages. The household's whole philosophy was vindicated: when everyone else is lawyering, the man who simply finishes inherits the road.
team.lares.objective: Keep the lamp lit, keep finishing, and let the arguing garages feed the record.
team.lares.definingEvent: The busiest winter of hospitality the hearth has ever hosted, courtesy of the hearing rooms.
team.lares.carryForward: A philosophy vindicated: when everyone else lawyers, the finisher inherits the road.

team.feet.summary: The grafters' opinion of the protest year fits on a bolt head: you cannot wrench a verdict. While the midfield queued at the hearing-room door, the working men kept boots on concrete and elbows out, J. Rampal dragging the FEET 13 places the stopwatch had no business allowing. The YOUGEN V10 ran honest for sixteen rounds, the garage hand-fettled everything, and the season's spoils were the honest kind: points taken from garages whose drivers were thinking about paperwork. Old Boots does not do drama. Old Boots does distances, and the distance, this year, paid better than the argument.
team.feet.objective: Out-work the argument and take the points the distracted garages leave lying.
team.feet.definingEvent: Sixteen rounds of boots-on-concrete while the midfield queued indoors.
team.feet.carryForward: Proof, banked, that the distance pays better than the argument.

team.serga.summary: The rich men's paper war was, to a garage with no lawyer, a spectator sport. E. Sambena kept the SERGA 1000 sharp on scavenging, the SC3000 F12 warming to its work, arguing about its oil, and the grey car kept embarrassing machinery whose owners were in hearings at the flag. Every point stayed banked, never bought. But the ledger kept its own score: rebuilds cost more than replacements when you cannot afford replacements, and midnight oil burns the man who burns it. Pride would not hear it; the spares shelf could count.
team.serga.objective: Watch the rich men's war through the fence and keep beating their cars with scrap.
team.serga.definingEvent: The ledger's quiet season: rebuilds costing more than replacements, and pride refusing to listen.
team.serga.carryForward: A spares shelf that can count, and a countdown nobody at Serga will say aloud.

team.cool.summary: The deckchairs have the finest view of the hearing-room queue, and the calmest garage in the circus enjoyed the show. A. Delvaux composed his love letters to the hairpins, drove when the COOL 05's antique heart agreed, and collected a point on an afternoon the midfield was indoors being examined. The CORSE V8 sulked in its electrics on schedule and ran sweetly off it, which the crew accepted like weather. The basement quarrel with Comet over who peaked hardest and fell furthest continued at the pace of all Cool things: slowly, cheerfully, unresolved.
team.cool.objective: Enjoy the show, collect what falls off the table, and resolve nothing.
team.cool.definingEvent: A point collected while the midfield queued for examination, celebrated at deckchair pace.
team.cool.carryForward: The quarrel with Comet, ongoing by mutual consent, and the deckchairs in the same spot.

team.moon.summary: From the far side of the grid, the paper war was distant weather: visible on the horizon, irrelevant to the orbit. K. Yepes kept circling, waving at the machines that lapped him, finishing when the bridge of a RAM V12 outlasted the eager machinery, which this attritional season was often. The pit wall's moonshots came to nothing, fondly. The points column stayed deep space. But the logbook holds the year's quiet truth: the silver car ran to more flags than ever before, and a team that finishes is, technically, rising. The telescope never moved.
team.moon.objective: Keep the orbit, outlast the eager, and log the slow proof of rising.
team.moon.definingEvent: More flags seen to the end than ever before, the logbook's quiet line of the year.
team.moon.carryForward: The same telescope, the same orbit, and a new line in the logbook called progress.

team.iris.summary: The white gloves stayed clean all season, because the jewel house files its arguments on the timing screens. B. Salgado's crown hunt ran its second year, the IRIS 717 knifing the sweepers, the PRISM 90 V10 singing its jewelled band, and the honest gap remained: race-day economy against the PALM 190, a beautiful argument short of a verdict. The paper war the house watched with distaste; jewellery is not made in hearing rooms. Across the family, Azalea collected another big afternoon. The treasury grew; the ledger against the crown did not, and the gloves began to clench.
team.iris.objective: Hunt the crown a second year and close the honest gap to the PALM 190.
team.iris.definingEvent: The gap named out loud in the winter debrief: economy, not speed, is the last wall.
team.iris.carryForward: Clenching gloves, a louder sister, and a gap now measured and targeted.

team.azalea.summary: The glacial one watched the paddock argue indoors and waited for the stages worth owning. They came on schedule: the big afternoons, the floodlit hairpins, the days that decided things, and the AZALEA 808 opened over all of them, the BLOOM 88 V8 building from a held breath to full song when the light was brightest. M. Larssen collected without celebration, because the win is the frost arriving. The sister war ran beneath the season, Iris loud and gleaming, Azalea cold and conclusive. The cabinet is not full; the grid checks the date of everything in it.
team.azalea.objective: Own the days that decide things and let the loud sister have the rest.
team.azalea.definingEvent: The floodlit afternoon the bloom opened over the establishment's decisive hour.
team.azalea.carryForward: A family with two jewels and one conclusion: the cold one collects what matters.
